item.name: Get Available Escorts (query params - use Params tab)
List Audits - Filter by outcome COMPLETED
List Audits - Filter by outcome NO_MATCH
Get Trip Assignment (shows primary driver/escort + confirmation state)

request.description: Returns the currently assigned driver_id and escort_id from Redis, plus driver_confirmed / escort_confirmed flags. Use this to identify who is the PRIMARY driver before triggering a rejection in ranking tests.

item.name: Get Trip Candidates (shows fallback queue — works for N drivers/escorts)

request.description: Returns the remaining fallback queues (fallback_drivers, fallback_escorts). These are ordered lists — the first entry is who gets offered next if the current primary rejects. When a rejection is processed the first entry is popped off this list. The list can contain any number of fallbacks; it is empty once all candidates have been tried.

item.name: 7. Ranking & Fallback Testing
item.description:
Tests for driver/escort rejection cascading to the next-best candidate, and for trip.no_match with AIC hotline when all candidates are exhausted.

PRE-REQUISITE: Use a fresh ranking_trip_id (variable: {{ranking_trip_id}}) so it doesn't clash with the Full Flow trip. The default is dddddddd-dddd-dddd-dddd-dddddddddddd.

Scenario A — Driver fallback (2 drivers, 1 escort): First driver rejects → re-offer to second driver → second driver accepts.
Scenario B — No drivers (1 driver, all reject) → trip.no_match with AIC hotline in audit.
Scenario C — Escort fallback (1 driver, 2 escorts): Escort 1 rejects → re-offer to Escort 2 → Escort 2 accepts.
Scenario D — No escorts (1 escort, rejects) → trip.no_match with AIC hotline in audit.

item.name: [A-4] Submit Trip (Tampines service area — uses ranking_trip_id)

exec: console.log('Wait ~3 seconds for Kafka to process, then run A-4b to identify the primary driver.');

item.name: [A-4b] Get Trip Assignment (identifies PRIMARY driver — run after ~3s)

exec: pm.test('Assignment exists with driver and escort', function() {
console.log('PRIMARY driver (offered, will reject in A-5):', json.driver_id);
console.log('WHY is escort not visible as a candidates:escort key?');
console.log('  candidates:escort holds FALLBACK escorts only.');
console.log('  With 1 escort registered: escort becomes primary → fallback queue is empty → Redis auto-deletes empty lists.');
console.log('  With 2+ escorts: candidates:escort shows the remaining fallbacks in offer order.');
console.log('TIP: Run GET /candidates to see remaining fallback queues for this trip.');

request.description: Reads trip:assignment:{trip_id} from Redis. driver_id = PRIMARY driver currently assigned and offered. escort_id = PRIMARY escort. The FALLBACK queue (candidates:driver key) holds any remaining drivers in offer order — check GET /candidates to see them.

item.name: [A-4c] Get Trip Candidates (see fallback queue — should show 1 fallback driver)

exec: pm.test('0 escort fallbacks (only 1 escort registered)', function() {
console.log('Fallback drivers (will be offered in this order if primary rejects):', json.fallback_drivers);
console.log('Fallback escorts:', json.fallback_escorts, '(empty — only 1 escort registered)');

request.description: Shows how many fallback drivers/escorts remain. This is a list — with 3+ drivers you would see [Driver2, Driver3, ...] ordered by match_score. Each rejection pops the first entry. When empty the system publishes trip.no_match.

item.name: [A-5] PRIMARY Driver Rejects (triggers fallback to other driver)

exec: console.log('Transport should now pop fallback driver and re-offer. Wait ~2s then check:');
console.log('  1. Kafdrop: new trip.offered.driver message with fallback_driver_id');
console.log('  2. RedisInsight: trip:assignment driver_id should now be', pm.collectionVariables.get('fallback_driver_id'));
console.log('  3. RedisInsight: candidates:driver:{id} should be gone (fallback was popped)');

item.name: [A-5b] Get Assignment After Rejection (identifies re-offered driver — wait ~2s first)

exec: pm.test('Assignment updated to next driver', function() {
pm.expect(json.driver_id).to.not.equal(oldPrimary, 'assignment should have changed to next driver');
// Save the NEW primary — whoever the system just re-offered.
// Works for any number of fallbacks: we always read from the assignment,
// not from a static list of IDs we created.
console.log('New primary (re-offered, use in A-6 accept):', json.driver_id);
console.log('If there were more drivers in the fallback queue, rejecting this one');
console.log('would cascade to the next — repeat A-5 → A-5b for each rejection.');

request.description: After the primary driver rejects, transport pops the next driver from candidates:driver and updates trip:assignment. This step reads the updated assignment to find out who was re-offered. Works regardless of how many fallbacks exist.
